recommended.title: Large Language Models in Peer Review: Efficiency vs. Bias
recommended.abstract: This paper explores the integration of LLMs into the peer review process, analyzing whether the increase in speed compensates for potential algorithmic biases in scholarly evaluation. We conduct a comprehensive study involving over 10,000 paper reviews and demonstrate that while LLMs can significantly reduce review time, they introduce subtle biases related to writing style and topic popularity that require careful mitigation strategies.

recommended.title: Quantum Neural Networks for Image Recognition
recommended.abstract: A novel approach to visual feature extraction using quantum-inspired kernels is proposed. We demonstrate that this method outperforms classical baselines in low-resource environments by up to 15% accuracy improvement. The proposed quantum neural network architecture can be implemented on near-term quantum devices with minimal quantum resources.

recommended.title: Sustainable Smart Cities: A Multi-modal Study
recommended.abstract: Smart city technologies often overlook the carbon footprint of their own infrastructure. This study provides a comprehensive framework for assessing the net environmental impact of IoT deployments in urban areas. We analyze data from 50 major cities and propose actionable recommendations for sustainable smart city development.
